text.title: MVP Scoping Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every MVP Scoping application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: MVP scope MUST cite ONE AHA-moment statement; multi-AHA-moments are rejected as scope creep.
rule.rationale: Multi-AHA-moments split focus and signal that the product hasn't been positioned.

rule.statement: Build window MUST be ≤4 calendar weeks; over-cap is rejected.
rule.rationale: Beyond 4 weeks the MVP becomes a small product and feedback delays compound.

rule.statement: Spec MUST carry an explicit cut-list (features removed with one-line rationale); implicit cuts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Explicit cut-list documents the discipline; implicit cuts re-emerge in scope creep.

rule.statement: Launch MUST be gated by a readiness checklist (instrumentation live, billing live, support inbox live, docs page live); failing checklist blocks launch.
rule.rationale: Un-gated launches ship broken; gated launches catch the obvious.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list ONE must-have user journey end-to-end; alternative journeys are park-listed.
